title: Making community data more accessible for all of Aotearoa New Zealand | Stats NZ
content:
## Making community data more accessible for all of Aotearoa New Zealand
26 November 2024, 10:45am
Stats NZ launched its online Place and ethnic group summaries tool today.
Place and ethnic group summaries are sets of user-friendly graphs and tables that cover a wide range of topics such as population, work, income, education, and housing.
“Place and ethnic group summaries is a valuable resource for our customers and communities,” general manager customer services and support Kathy Connolly said. “It allows customers to paint a picture of their community, understand how it is changing over time, and compare their community with others.”

## Compare your community
Use Place and ethnic group summaries to find out how your geographic community compares with other communities in New Zealand. For example, how does your community compare with:
Rate of home ownership
Rate of home ownership
94.1 percent of households in Otatara (Invercargill)
94.1 percent of households in Otatara (Invercargill)
Median weekly rent paid by households
Median weekly rent paid by households
$925 in Long Bay (Auckland)
$925 in Long Bay (Auckland)
Proportion of people who bike to work
Proportion of people who bike to work
19.7 percent of people who live in Beckenham (Christchurch)
19.7 percent of people who live in Beckenham (Christchurch)
Median household income
Median household income
$188,200 in Wadestown (Wellington)
$188,200 in Wadestown (Wellington)
Proportion of people who speak New Zealand Sign Language (NZSL)
Proportion of people who speak New Zealand Sign Language (NZSL)
1.7 percent of people who live in Dallington (Christchurch)
1.7 percent of people who live in Dallington (Christchurch)
1. All comparisons are at the statistical area 3 geographic level.
1. All comparisons are at the statistical area 3 geographic level.
You can also use Place and ethnic group summaries to find out how your ethnic group compares with others. For example:
- proportion of people who moved residence between the North and South islands since the 2018 Census: 8.9 percent of Uruguayans
- proportion of people who can speak more than one language: 78.0 percent of Hong Kong Chinese.
Place and ethnic group summaries includes data from the 2023 Census as well as other data published by Stats NZ on topics such as business demography, life expectancy, and child poverty.
“Place and ethnic group summaries will be updated with the latest non-census data approximately every six months,” Connolly said.
Place and ethnic group summaries user guide has more information about the tool, including instructional videos.
This tool complements other 2023 Census products that publish data at different levels of detail. 2023 Census products and services has a full list of the products available.
To view iwi and Māori descent data from 2023 Census, visit Te Whata . Developed by Te Kāhui Raraunga, Te Whata is a data platform tailored specifically by iwi for iwi.
Sandi Reily 021 285 9191 media@stats.govt.nz
